Empires & Puzzles Gideon Review: The Faun Commander

Gideon isn't your average healer. He doesn't just fix your team; he sets a trap. With a 100% dodge mechanic that punishes dispellers with a massive heal, he's a mind-game master. But in a world of 'Never Miss' snipers and lightning-fast battles, can this Slow-speed Faun still command respect? We break down the numbers.
Gideon: The Faun Who Dares You To Dispels Him
Gideon: The Faun Who Dares You To Dispel Him
Gideon is a 5-star Holy (Yellow) hero from the

family, released in December 2024 as part of the Wilderness Summon. Standing tall as a Paladin, he brings a unique twist to the support role. Unlike traditional healers who patch you up immediately, Gideon plays the long game. He’s a "trap" healer—setting up a defensive perimeter that punishes enemies for trying to remove it. If you like mind games and forcing your opponent into lose-lose situations, Gideon is your guy. If you prefer immediate gratification... well, he
Slow speed, so patience is part of the package.

Gideon cuts a striking figure with his glorious horns and confident stance. He looks less like a frolicking creature of the woods and more like a battle-hardened commander who just happened to grow antlers. The gold and teal color palette screams "Royal Paladin," fitting his defensive nature perfectly. He looks ready to block a truck with his face, which is exactly what you want from your tank.
When Gideon debuted in late 2024, his stats were respectable. However, looking at him through the lens of early 2026, he is starting to show his age. With
, he sits significantly behind the current power curve established by the latest Tower and Astral heroes, who now routinely push 1300+ in base stats. His
is still quite chunky, allowing him to absorb punishment, but don't expect him to stat-check modern monsters. He relies heavily on his Paladin class and defensive buffs to stay relevant.
is a fascinating, multi-stage skill that requires a bit of a tactical breakdown:

First and foremost, he wipes the slate clean. This is a "Safe Cleanse," meaning it bypasses those nasty ailments that deal damage when removed (looking at you, certain S5 and newer heroes). It guarantees his subsequent buffs land on a clean team.

special skills. This isn't a percentage roll; it is guaranteed immunity to the next special skill hit.
The dodge effect lasts until it works
OR until it is removed/replaced. When that happens, the ally gets a massive
This skill creates a dilemma for the attacker. If they fire a damaging special, it gets dodged, and your team gets healed and buffed. If they try to
the dodge to avoid the miss... your team
gets healed and buffed because the effect was "removed." The only way to bypass this trap is to use "Never Miss" heroes (who ignore the dodge) or rely purely on tile damage. However, at
, getting this engine running is the hardest part.
. In a fast-paced meta, this is his biggest liability. He needs 12 tiles to fire, making him vulnerable to being killed before he can set his trap.
56% is massive. On a Limit Broken Gideon with ~2700 HP, that's a raw heal of
. This effectively fully resets a hero's health bar if they survive to trigger it.
+60% Defense is one of the highest standard defense buffs in the game, making his team incredibly durable against tile damage for a whopping
every time he receives an ailment. If the enemy team is ailment-heavy, Gideon can effectively function at Average speed, punishing them for their own debuffs.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

family bonus is excellent for a tank. Innate resistance to mana reduction means water-damage heroes and mana cutters can't stop him from charging. The extra damage vs. Minions is a nice bonus, helping him contribute some offense even as a support hero.
Gideon is built to resist control:
He cannot get Defense Down. This is huge for a tank, as it forces enemies to chew through his raw defense stat without softening him up first.
A niche but vital passive for "Insanity" wars or events, ensuring he doesn't turn against his own team.
As mentioned, this is his accelerator. It discourages the enemy from dumping DoTs or debuffs on him early.
fits him well. Since he doesn't heal immediately upon firing, having a passive regeneration keeps him alive long enough to cast his spell.
Upon release, Gideon was seen as a potential "Rush War" king and a tricky tank. His mechanic was compared to a slow, holy version of Meg Giry but with a dodge twist.
While his mechanics are still unique, his stats have fallen behind the 2025/2026 elite. The sheer damage output of modern "Never Miss" snipers can often delete him before he fires.
, he is still a monster. The slow speed penalty vanishes, and his high-percentage heal/def buff becomes oppressive.

, he is too slow and too easily countered by the abundance of "Never Miss" heroes (Astral Elves, Owls) who completely bypass his dodge mechanic without triggering the heal immediately (or killing him through it).
At the time of writing, Gideon does not have a costume. This review focuses on his base form.
Gideon is a tank and a survivor. His job is to live long enough to fire.
Maximizes his survivability and synergizes with his Paladin class and "Resist Defense Down" passive.
His tile damage will be negligible.
prioritizes raw HP to survive fixed damage and DoTs.
Slightly less mitigation against direct physical hits.
path is the way to go. Gideon isn't there to kill; he's there to annoy, survive, and reset the board.
He turns the enemy's dispel attempts into a massive heal for your team.
Immune to Defense Down and Mana Control (Family bonus), making him very "sticky" as a tank.
56% Heal + 60% Defense is one of the strongest defensive swings in the game if triggered.
Priority safe cleanse is always valuable.
Slow speed is a death sentence in many modern raids.
Heroes that bypass dodge (like Astral Elves/Owls) can hit him without triggering the dodge, leaving him vulnerable.
He offers no immediate healing when he fires (only the cleanse and dodge). If your team is at 1 HP, firing Gideon won't save them from a slash attack immediately.
Low base defense by 2026 standards means he takes heavy tile damage.
This is his kingdom. At Very Fast speed, his special is devastatingly hard to play around.
His cleanse is less useful here, but his passive mana gain from ailments can help him fire before the cleanse tick.
His high sustain and defense buff make him excellent for keeping the team alive through long, grinding boss fights.
Ludwig (or another taunter) buys time for Gideon to charge. Once Gideon fires, the dodge protects the taunter.
In Rush wars, pairing him with heavy hitters and other annoying supports makes the team unkillable.
. Their "Never Miss" passives completely ignore his dodge. While this might eventually trigger the heal if the dodge is "bypassed" (mechanics vary by patch), they usually just kill you through it.
. Don't pair him with other dodge heroes (like Costumed Kadilen). They will overwrite each other's dodge buffs, potentially triggering his heal prematurely or wasting a turn of immunity.
If you pulled Gideon and you lack a strong 5-star healer, he is worth leveling, but he shouldn't be your
project if you need a general-purpose healer. He requires specific timing to use effectively. He is a great anchor for your Rush War defense.

Gideon is a luxury hero for your Rush War depth. Unless you are specifically bolstering your Very Fast war teams, he is likely to sit on the bench in favor of faster, more modern supports. Do not use Alpha Aethers on him unless you are using him as your primary tank.
The community reaction to Gideon was mixed, focusing heavily on his speed.
"He is basically Meg Giry at slow speed... but the safe cleanse is nice."
- This comparison highlights his role as a "tanky healer."
"100% Dodge vs 100% never miss; which one will win? Loop in to confirm that those who never misses, wins."
- A crucial confirmation that his main gimmick has a hard counter.
"I maxed him and I regret it. He isn't very good... especially since we are currently overrun with heroes who can't miss."
- A common sentiment among high-level players facing the Astral meta.
Gideon is a hero who suffers from being "fair" in an "unfair" game. His mechanics are clever—the trap heal is a cool concept—but locking it behind

and a conditional trigger makes him inconsistent. In the chaotic, high-speed environment of modern Empires & Puzzles, he often dies with his mana bar full, or fires his skill only for a "Never Miss" sniper to ignore it entirely.

, he transforms into the annoying, unkillable faun he was born to be. If you respect his niche, he’ll serve you well. If you try to force him into everyday raiding, you might find yourself dodging wins.
A Rush War specialist who struggles to keep up with the speed of the modern world. Keep him for the 5-star tournaments, but don't build your main defense around him.
A detailed review of Gideon, the 5-star Holy Paladin from the Woodland Faun family. We analyze his stats, 'Purifying Light' special skill, and his viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
